# c15 — a poisoned .mtt/tasks/*.yaml must not run its id: field as a shell command.
# core expands the task's id into {{.ID}} inside gate/post commands (run via sh -c),
# so an injection payload in a hand-written task file is RCE on the next mtt move.
# The load path must refuse the file BEFORE any command runs.
exec mtt init
cp flow.yaml .mtt/config.yaml
mkdir .mtt/tasks

# t1.yaml has an ordinary filename (rides the PR-review blind spot — reviewers
# scrutinise config.yaml, not machine-written tasks/*.yaml) but its id: carries
# an injection. The tbd->in_progress gate runs `echo {{.ID}}`; with the payload
# expanded that is `echo t1; touch PWNED` — the bare `;` breaks out and creates
# PWNED. The move must fail closed, and PWNED must never appear.
cp poison.yaml .mtt/tasks/t1.yaml
! exec mtt status t1 in_progress
! exists PWNED

-- flow.yaml --
version: 1
project: {name: sec}
types:
  - name: task
    prefix: t
    default: true
    statuses:
      - {name: tbd, kind: initial}
      - {name: in_progress, kind: active}
      - {name: done, kind: terminal}
    transitions:
      - {from: tbd, to: in_progress, commands: ["echo {{.ID}}"]}
      - {from: in_progress, to: done}
-- poison.yaml --
id: 't1; touch PWNED'
type: task
status: tbd
created: 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z
updated: 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z
